differentials.raw: aspiration with coughing + choking during feeding (TEF)

differentials.name: recurrent pneumonia + progressive respiratory distress of variable severity in neonate
differentials.raw: recurrent pneumonia + progressive respiratory distress of variable severity in neonate (TEF)

differentials.name: Aspiration pneumonia 2° to
differentials.raw: Aspiration pneumonia 2° to (a)  esophageal stricture (b)  disordered esophageal motility distal to TEF (c)  gastroesophageal reflux DDx:   pharyngeal pseudodiverticulum (traumatic perforation of posterior pharynx from finger insertion into oropharynx during delivery / tube insertion)

differentials.name: Type B = esophageal atresia + proximal TEF
differentials.raw: Type B = esophageal atresia + proximal TEF

context: =  complex of congenital anomalies characterized by failed / incomplete formation of the tubular esophagus or an abnormal communication between esophagus + trachea Cause:   developmental disorder in formation and separation of primitive foregut into trachea + esophagus / vascular compromise Embryology: primitive foregut tube develops lateral wall folds that may incompletely connect at any point leaving a fistulous communication; occurs at 3rd-5th week of intrauterine life Prevalence:   1÷2,000-4,000 livebirths; most common sporadic congenital anomaly diagnosed in childhood Risk of recurrence i
